_note: Atlanta HQ data — Mercedes-Benz Stadium, downtown Atlanta. 5 group stage matches + R32 + R16 + Semifinal.

venue.name: Mercedes-Benz Stadium
venue.notes: Retractable roof — climate controlled regardless of Georgia summer heat. Clear bag policy enforced. Famous for fan-friendly concession pricing.
venue.parkingNote: Downtown decks (Home Depot Backyard, Red/Gold decks) from $40. Book via the official FIFA app. MARTA is strongly preferred on match days.

transit.recommended: MARTA rail — GWCC/CNN Center station (Blue/Green lines) and Vine City station are both steps from the stadium
lines: Blue & Green lines — GWCC/CNN Center and Vine City stations adjacent to the stadium
Red & Gold lines — transfer at Five Points, one stop away
tips: Buy a Breeze card before match day — station lines get long 2+ hours pre-kickoff.
Five Points transfer station is the crowd pinch point — route via Vine City if coming from the west.
Trains run extended hours on match nights; confirm last train time for late kickoffs.
Midtown and Decatur bars are a straight MARTA ride from the stadium — easy post-match exit.
transit.parking: Decks within 3 blocks run $40–$90 on match days. The Home Depot Backyard lot doubles as the tailgate zone — arrives full 3 hours early.
transit.rideshare: Designated pickup on Northside Drive, west of the stadium. Expect heavy surge post-match — MARTA beats rideshare on every Atlanta match day.

fanZones.name: FIFA Fan Festival — Centennial Olympic Park
fanZones.notes: Free entry, a 10-minute walk from the stadium. Giant screens, live music, food vendors — the legacy 1996 Olympics park becomes the South's biggest watch party.

fanZones.name: The Home Depot Backyard
fanZones.notes: Atlanta's signature tailgate lawn. Expect a World Cup twist on the Falcons/Atlanta United tailgate tradition. Capacity limits possible — arrive early.

preGame.name: Castleberry Hill
preGame.notes: Atlanta's walkable pre-match district. Galleries, lofts, and bars along Walker St and Peters St fill with supporters' groups hours before kickoff.

preGame.name: Marietta Street corridor
preGame.notes: Stadium-adjacent strip of breweries and sports bars between the park and the venue. The default meeting point for neutral fans.

preGame.name: West Midtown breweries
preGame.notes: Monday Night Garage and the Westside brewery scene host watch parties all tournament. Relaxed option before evening kickoffs.

cityEnergyForecast.crowdNote: Spain vs Cape Verde — Atlanta's first heavyweight group-stage date. Spain supporters travel, and even a Monday lunchtime kickoff should pull a big neutral crowd.
Czechia vs South Africa — less star power, but two traveling fan bases and a weekday downtown slot should still give Atlanta a lively crowd.
Spain vs Saudi Arabia — Sunday noon with Spain back under the roof. Strong family turnout, plenty of neutrals, and a more polished football crowd.
Morocco vs Haiti — evening kickoff with real upset energy. Morocco's recent World Cup pedigree plus Haiti's underdog support should create real noise.
DR Congo vs Uzbekistan — Group K closer on a Saturday night. Not the glamour ticket, but diaspora turnout and qualification stakes should lift the atmosphere.

transitPain.label: Moderate
transitPain.note: Spain vs Cape Verde — Monday noon kickoff hits lunch-hour and early-afternoon downtown traffic. Use MARTA and arrive early.
tips: Ideal dry run if you're attending multiple Atlanta matches
GWCC/CNN Center station will be comfortable — no need to reroute
Downtown lunch traffic is the only real factor

transitPain.label: Moderate
transitPain.note: Czechia vs South Africa — Thursday noon is easier than a night game, but downtown work traffic still complicates driving.
tips: MARTA still beats driving into a weekday downtown lunch window
Vine City station is the calmer entry point
Post-match trains should clear faster than Atlanta's night matches

transitPain.label: Low
transitPain.note: Spain vs Saudi Arabia — Sunday noon is manageable, but Spain's draw raises the walk-up crowd.
tips: Easiest driving day if you must park — decks won't fill
Combine with the Centennial Park Fan Festival before kickoff
Post-match rideshare wait under 15 min expected

transitPain.label: High
transitPain.note: Morocco vs Haiti — Wednesday evening kickoff overlaps with commute and downtown event traffic.
tips: Be inside the stadium perimeter by 5pm or budget serious buffer
Five Points transfer will be jammed — ride Blue/Green direct if possible
Book post-match plans in Castleberry Hill, not Midtown, to stay walkable

transitPain.label: High
transitPain.note: DR Congo vs Uzbekistan — Saturday night closer with qualification stakes. Downtown will be busy, but less all-consuming than a blue-blood showdown.
tips: Arrive 2+ hours early and make a full evening of it
Fan Festival at Centennial Park will be at capacity by late afternoon
Pre-book any post-match transport; surge pricing will be extreme
Last MARTA trains will be packed — leave on the early side or settle in downtown

shouldIGoScores.label: Consider
shouldIGoScores.summary: Spain vs Cape Verde — the biggest name in Atlanta's first match, but a Monday noon kickoff keeps it more practical than prestige-first. Solid opener if you want Spain without prime-time pricing.

shouldIGoScores.label: Go
shouldIGoScores.summary: Czechia vs South Africa — midday match, lower secondary-market pressure, and a genuinely open group dynamic. Good value if you want a World Cup day without paying Spain prices.

shouldIGoScores.label: Consider
shouldIGoScores.summary: Spain vs Saudi Arabia — Spain are the draw, the roof keeps conditions perfect, and Sunday noon is friendlier than Atlanta's weekday crunch. A cleaner experience than the later-night slates.

shouldIGoScores.label: Strongly Go
shouldIGoScores.summary: Morocco vs Haiti — one of Atlanta's more intriguing group-stage tickets. Morocco bring recent World Cup credibility, Haiti bring emotion, and the evening slot should feel lively without absolute peak pricing.

shouldIGoScores.label: Go
shouldIGoScores.summary: DR Congo vs Uzbekistan — late-group stakes on a Saturday night. Not the marquee name matchup, but likely one of Atlanta's better value tickets if you want a real result with something on the line.
